content.content:
1	"""
2	Matter lock provider.
3	
4	Handles PIN credential management via Matter lock helpers.
5	PINs are write-only: occupied slots report ``SlotCredential.unreadable()``,
6	cleared slots report ``SlotCredential.empty()``. Subscribes to DoorLock
7	cluster events via the push framework for code slot tracking (LockOperation)
8	and occupancy updates (LockUserChange).
9	"""
10	
11	from __future__ import annotations
12	
13	from dataclasses import dataclass
14	from datetime import timedelta
15	from typing import Any, Literal
16	
17	from matter_server.common.models import EventType
18	
19	from homeassistant.components.matter.helpers import (
20	    get_matter,
21	    get_node_from_device_entry,
22	)
23	from homeassistant.components.matter.lock_helpers import (
24	    SetCredentialFailedError,
25	    clear_lock_credential,
26	    clear_lock_user,
27	    get_lock_credential_status,
28	    get_lock_info,
29	    get_lock_users,
30	    set_lock_credential,
31	    set_lock_user,
32	)
33	from homeassistant.config_entries import ConfigEntry
34	from homeassistant.core import callback
35	from homeassistant.exceptions import HomeAssistantError, ServiceValidationError
36	
37	from ..domain.credentials import (
38	    Credential,
39	    CredentialRef,
40	    CredentialType,
41	    CredentialTypeCapability,
42	    LockCapabilities,
43	    SetUserResult,
44	    User,
45	)
46	from ..domain.exceptions import (
47	    CodeRejectedError,
48	    DuplicateCodeError,
49	    LockCodeManagerProviderError,
50	    LockDisconnected,
51	    LockOperationFailed,
52	)
53	from ..domain.models import SlotCredential
54	from ._base import BaseLock
55	from ._util import parse_slot_num
56	from .const import LOGGER
57	
58	# DoorLock cluster ID (0x0101 = 257)
59	_DOOR_LOCK_CLUSTER_ID = 257
60	
61	# DoorLock cluster event IDs
62	_LOCK_OPERATION_EVENT_ID = 2
63	_LOCK_USER_CHANGE_EVENT_ID = 4
64	
65	# LockUserChange LockDataType values
66	_LOCK_DATA_TYPE_PIN = 6
67	
68	# LockUserChange DataOperationType values
69	_DATA_OP_ADD = 0
70	_DATA_OP_CLEAR = 1
71	_DATA_OP_MODIFY = 2
72	
73	
74	@dataclass(repr=False, eq=False)
75	class MatterLock(BaseLock):
76	    """Class to represent a Matter lock."""
77	
78	    @property
79	    def domain(self) -> str:
80	        """Return integration domain."""
81	        return "matter"
82	
83	    @property
84	    def supports_push(self) -> bool:
85	        """
86	        Return whether this lock supports push-based updates.
87	
88	        Matter locks push occupancy changes via LockUserChange events.
89	        PINs are still write-only (values are never pushed), but slot
90	        occupancy (unreadable/empty credentials) is pushed in real time.
91	        """
92	        return True
93	
94	    @property
95	    def usercode_scan_interval(self) -> timedelta:
96	        """Return scan interval for usercodes."""
97	        return timedelta(minutes=5)
98	
99	    @property
100	    def hard_refresh_interval(self) -> timedelta | None:
101	        """
102	        Return interval between hard refreshes for drift detection.
103	
104	        Matter locks support push events for local changes, but API-initiated
105	        changes bypass push notifications. Periodic hard refresh catches drift
106	        from external tools or missed events.
107	        """
108	        return timedelta(hours=1)
109	
110	    @property
111	    def supports_native_users(self) -> bool:
112	        """
113	        Return True — Matter locks expose the full User/Credential model.
114	
115	        Matter's DoorLock cluster manages users and credentials as distinct
116	        entities, so the base orchestration runs the user-first lifecycle
117	        (create/update user, then write its Personal Identification Number
118	        credential; delete the user when its last credential is removed).
119	        """
120	        return True
121	
122	    def _get_matter_node(self) -> Any | None:
123	        """
124	        Get the MatterNode for this lock from the Matter integration.
125	
126	        Uses the Matter integration's helper to resolve the node from the
127	        device entry, which correctly handles the device identifier format.
128	        Returns the node object with .node_id and access to the client.
129	        """
130	        if not self.device_entry:
131	            return None
132	        try:
133	            return get_node_from_device_entry(self.hass, self.device_entry)
134	        except Exception as err:
135	            LOGGER.debug(
136	                "Failed to resolve Matter node for %s: %s",
137	                self.lock.entity_id,
138	                err,
139	            )
140	            return None
141	
142	    def _get_matter_client(self) -> Any | None:
143	        """Get the MatterClient via the Matter integration helper."""
144	        try:
145	            return get_matter(self.hass).matter_client
146	        except Exception as err:
147	            LOGGER.debug(
148	                "Failed to get Matter client for %s: %s",
149	                self.lock.entity_id,
150	                err,
151	            )
152	            return None
153	
154	    def _require_client_and_node(self) -> tuple[Any, Any]:
155	        """Get client and node, raising LockDisconnected if unavailable."""
156	        client = self._get_matter_client()
157	        node = self._get_matter_node()
158	        if not client or not node:
159	            raise LockDisconnected(
160	                f"Matter client or node unavailable for {self.lock.entity_id}"
161	            )
162	        return client, node
163	
164	    # -- Credential primitives -----------------------------------------------
165	
166	    async def async_get_users(self) -> list[User]:
167	        """
168	        Read every user and their Personal Identification Number credentials from the lock.
169	
170	        Matter PINs are write-only: each occupied credential slot is projected to
171	        SlotCredential.unreadable(). Non-PIN credentials (for example RFID) are
172	        filtered out because the coordinator and sync manager only manage PIN slots.
173	        """
174	        client, node = self._require_client_and_node()
175	        try:
176	            lock_data = await get_lock_users(client, node)
177	        except HomeAssistantError as err:
178	            raise LockDisconnected(
179	                f"Matter get_lock_users failed for {self.lock.entity_id}: {err}"
180	            ) from err
181	
182	        # A for-loop (not a comprehension) so the int-or-None user_index and
183	        # credential index are narrowed by explicit guards before use: the
184	        # Matter helper types both as ``int | None``.
185	        users: list[User] = []
186	        for raw_user in lock_data.get("users", []):
187	            user_index = raw_user.get("user_index")
188	            if user_index is None:
189	                continue
190	            pin_credentials: list[Credential] = []
191	            for cred in raw_user.get("credentials", []):
192	                slot = cred.get("index")
193	                if cred.get("type") != "pin" or slot is None:
194	                    continue
195	                pin_credentials.append(
196	                    Credential(
197	                        type=CredentialType.PIN,
198	                        slot=slot,
199	                        state=SlotCredential.unreadable(),
200	                    )
201	                )
202	            users.append(
203	                User(
204	                    user_id=user_index,
205	                    name=raw_user.get("user_name"),
206	                    active=True,
207	                    credentials=pin_credentials,
208	                )
209	            )
210	        return users
211	
212	    async def async_get_capabilities(self) -> LockCapabilities:
213	        """
214	        Read lock capabilities from the Matter DoorLock cluster.
215	
216	        Maps the get_lock_info result to a platform-neutral LockCapabilities.
217	        Only the Personal Identification Number credential type is surfaced
218	        when the lock advertises PIN support. None capacity fields default
219	        to 0 (unknown capacity) rather than raising.
220	        """
221	        client, node = self._require_client_and_node()
222	        try:
223	            info = await get_lock_info(client, node)
224	        except HomeAssistantError as err:
225	            raise LockDisconnected(
226	                f"Matter get_lock_info failed for {self.lock.entity_id}: {err}"
227	            ) from err
228	
229	        credential_types: dict[CredentialType, CredentialTypeCapability] = {}
230	        if "pin" in (info.get("supported_credential_types") or []):
231	            credential_types[CredentialType.PIN] = CredentialTypeCapability(
232	                num_slots=info.get("max_pin_users") or 0,
233	                min_length=info.get("min_pin_length") or 0,
234	                max_length=info.get("max_pin_length") or 0,
235	                supports_learn=False,
236	            )
237	
238	        return LockCapabilities(
239	            supports_user_management=bool(info.get("supports_user_management")),
240	            max_users=info.get("max_users") or 0,
241	            credential_types=credential_types,
242	        )
243	
244	    async def async_set_user(self, user: User) -> SetUserResult:
245	        """
246	        Create or update a lock user, returning whether it was newly created.
247	
248	        The user.user_id here is the slot (credential_index). Matter allocates
249	        user_index independently: you cannot create a user at a chosen index —
250	        set_lock_user raises UserSlotEmptyError when user_index is given for an
251	        empty slot. So the flow is:
252	
253	        1. Check the credential slot's current owner via get_lock_credential_status.
254	        2. If the slot is occupied (UPDATE): call set_lock_user with the existing
255	           user_index from the status response.
256	        3. If the slot is empty (CREATE): call set_lock_user with user_index=None
257	           so Matter auto-allocates a free user slot and returns the new index.
258	
259	        The returned SetUserResult carries the real Matter user_index, which the
260	        base orchestration passes into async_set_credential as the user_id.
261	        """
262	        client, node = self._require_client_and_node()
263	        try:
264	            status = await get_lock_credential_status(
265	                client,
266	                node,
267	                credential_type="pin",
268	                credential_index=user.user_id,
269	            )
270	        except HomeAssistantError as err:
271	            raise LockDisconnected(
272	                f"Matter get_lock_credential_status failed for "
273	                f"{self.lock.entity_id}: {err}"
274	            ) from err
275	
276	        credential_exists = status.get("credential_exists", False)
277	        existing_user_index = status.get("user_index")
278	
279	        if credential_exists and existing_user_index is not None:
280	            # UPDATE: the slot is occupied — modify the existing user record.
281	            try:
282	                await set_lock_user(
283	                    client,
284	                    node,
285	                    user_index=existing_user_index,
286	                    user_name=user.name,
287	                )
288	            except ServiceValidationError as err:
289	                raise LockOperationFailed(
290	                    f"Matter set_lock_user rejected input for "
291	                    f"{self.lock.entity_id}: {err}"
292	                ) from err
293	            except HomeAssistantError as err:
294	                raise LockDisconnected(
295	                    f"Matter set_lock_user failed for {self.lock.entity_id}: {err}"
296	                ) from err
297	            return SetUserResult(user_id=existing_user_index, created=False)
298	
299	        # CREATE: the slot is empty — let Matter auto-allocate a user_index.
300	        try:
301	            result = await set_lock_user(
302	                client,
303	                node,
304	                user_index=None,
305	                user_name=user.name,
306	            )
307	        except ServiceValidationError as err:
308	            raise LockOperationFailed(
309	                f"Matter set_lock_user rejected input for {self.lock.entity_id}: {err}"
310	            ) from err
311	        except HomeAssistantError as err:
312	            raise LockDisconnected(
313	                f"Matter set_lock_user failed for {self.lock.entity_id}: {err}"
314	            ) from err
315	        return SetUserResult(user_id=result["user_index"], created=True)
316	
317	    async def async_delete_user(self, user_id: int) -> None:
318	        """
319	        Delete a lock user and all of its credentials.
320	
321	        The Matter DoorLock ClearUser command also clears all associated
322	        credentials and schedules for the user per the Matter specification.
323	        """
324	        client, node = self._require_client_and_node()
325	        try:
326	            await clear_lock_user(client, node, user_id)
327	        except HomeAssistantError as err:
328	            raise LockDisconnected(
329	                f"Matter clear_lock_user failed for {self.lock.entity_id}: {err}"
330	            ) from err
331	
332	    async def _send_set_credential(
333	        self,
334	        client: Any,
335	        node: Any,
336	        code_slot: int,
337	        pin: str,
338	        user_id: int,
339	    ) -> None:
340	        """
341	        Send set_lock_credential to the lock for the given slot, PIN, and user.
342	
343	        Raises SetCredentialFailedError on lock rejection,
344	        CodeRejectedError on validation failure,
345	        LockDisconnected on communication failure.
346	        """
347	        try:
348	            await set_lock_credential(
349	                client,
350	                node,
351	                credential_type="pin",
352	                credential_data=pin,
353	                credential_index=code_slot,
354	                user_index=user_id,
355	            )
356	        except SetCredentialFailedError:
357	            raise
358	        except ServiceValidationError as err:
359	            # Bad Personal Identification Number / unsupported type -> the lock
360	            # rejects the value; surface as a code rejection.
361	            raise CodeRejectedError(
362	                code_slot=code_slot,
363	                lock_entity_id=self.lock.entity_id,
364	                reason=str(err),
365	            ) from err
366	        except HomeAssistantError as err:
367	            # Transport / endpoint failure -> route to the retry path.
368	            raise LockDisconnected(
369	                f"Matter set_lock_credential failed for {self.lock.entity_id}: {err}"
370	            ) from err
371	
372	    async def async_set_credential(
373	        self,
374	        user_id: int,
375	        credential: Credential,
376	        *,
377	        name: str | None,
378	        source: Literal["sync", "direct"],
379	    ) -> bool:
380	        """
381	        Write a Personal Identification Number credential to the lock.
382	
383	        Raises CodeRejectedError when the credential has no readable value
384	        (for example when projecting an already-unreadable slot — the lock
385	        requires a concrete PIN string). Handles the duplicate-slot restart
386	        case for sync sources by clearing and retrying once.
387	
388	        The base orchestration skips coordinator refresh for push providers.
389	        Matter does not emit LockUserChange for LCM-initiated writes, so an
390	        optimistic push is required to keep the coordinator current.
391	        """
392	        if credential.readable_pin is None:
393	            raise CodeRejectedError(
394	                code_slot=credential.slot,
395	                lock_entity_id=self.lock.entity_id,
396	                reason="credential has no readable Personal Identification Number value",
397	            )
398	
399	        client, node = self._require_client_and_node()
400	        pin = credential.readable_pin
401	        slot = credential.slot
402	
403	        try:
404	            await self._send_set_credential(client, node, slot, pin, user_id)
405	        except SetCredentialFailedError as err:
406	            status = (err.translation_placeholders or {}).get("status", "")
407	            if status != "duplicate":
408	                raise CodeRejectedError(
409	                    code_slot=slot,
410	                    lock_entity_id=self.lock.entity_id,
411	                    reason=str(err),
412	                ) from err
413	            if source != "sync":
414	                raise DuplicateCodeError(
415	                    code_slot=slot,
416	                    lock_entity_id=self.lock.entity_id,
417	                ) from err
418	
419	            # Sync duplicate: clear and retry once
420	            LOGGER.debug(
421	                "Lock %s: duplicate on slot %s, clearing and retrying",
422	                self.lock.entity_id,
423	                slot,
424	            )
425	            await clear_lock_credential(
426	                client, node, credential_type="pin", credential_index=slot
427	            )
428	            try:
429	                await self._send_set_credential(client, node, slot, pin, user_id)
430	            except SetCredentialFailedError as retry_err:
431	                retry_status = (retry_err.translation_placeholders or {}).get(
432	                    "status", ""
433	                )
434	                if retry_status == "duplicate":
435	                    raise DuplicateCodeError(
436	                        code_slot=slot,
437	                        lock_entity_id=self.lock.entity_id,
438	                    ) from retry_err
439	                raise CodeRejectedError(
440	                    code_slot=slot,
441	                    lock_entity_id=self.lock.entity_id,
442	                    reason=str(retry_err),
443	                ) from retry_err
444	
445	        self._push_credential_update(slot, SlotCredential.unreadable())
446	        return True
447	
448	    async def async_delete_credential(self, ref: CredentialRef) -> bool:
449	        """
450	        Clear a Personal Identification Number credential from the lock.
451	
452	        Returns True on success. Pushes SlotCredential.empty() to the
453	        coordinator immediately because Matter does not emit LockUserChange
454	        for LCM-initiated clears.
455	        """
456	        client, node = self._require_client_and_node()
457	        try:
458	            await clear_lock_credential(
459	                client,
460	                node,
461	                credential_type="pin",
462	                credential_index=ref.slot,
463	            )
464	        except ServiceValidationError as err:
465	            raise LockOperationFailed(
466	                f"Matter clear_lock_credential rejected input for "
467	                f"{self.lock.entity_id}: {err}"
468	            ) from err
469	        except HomeAssistantError as err:
470	            raise LockDisconnected(
471	                f"Matter clear_lock_credential failed for {self.lock.entity_id}: {err}"
472	            ) from err
473	
474	        self._push_credential_update(ref.slot, SlotCredential.empty())
475	        return True
476	
477	    async def async_setup(self, config_entry: ConfigEntry) -> None:
478	        """Validate the lock supports Matter user management."""
479	        client, node = self._require_client_and_node()
480	        try:
481	            lock_info = await get_lock_info(client, node)
482	        except ServiceValidationError as err:
483	            raise LockCodeManagerProviderError(
484	                f"Matter get_lock_info rejected input for {self.lock.entity_id}: {err}"
485	            ) from err
486	        except HomeAssistantError as err:
487	            raise LockDisconnected(
488	                f"Matter get_lock_info failed for {self.lock.entity_id}: {err}"
489	            ) from err
490	        if not lock_info.get("supports_user_management"):
491	            raise LockCodeManagerProviderError(
492	                f"Matter lock {self.lock.entity_id} does not support user management"
493	            )
494	        if "pin" not in (lock_info.get("supported_credential_types") or []):
495	            raise LockCodeManagerProviderError(
496	                f"Matter lock {self.lock.entity_id} does not support PIN credentials"
497	            )
498	        LOGGER.debug(
499	            "Matter lock %s setup complete: %s",
500	            self.lock.entity_id,
501	            lock_info,
502	        )
503	
504	    async def async_is_device_available(self) -> bool:
505	        """Return whether the Matter lock device is available for commands."""
506	        try:
507	            client, node = self._require_client_and_node()
508	            await get_lock_info(client, node)
509	        except (LockCodeManagerProviderError, HomeAssistantError) as err:
510	            LOGGER.debug(
511	                "Lock %s: availability check failed: %s",
512	                self.lock.entity_id,
513	                err,
514	            )
515	            return False
516	        return True
517	
518	    # -- Event subscription via push framework --------------------------------
519	
520	    @callback
521	    def setup_push_subscription(self) -> None:
522	        """
523	        Subscribe to Matter DoorLock cluster events.
524	
525	        Handles two event types:
526	        - LockOperation (event 2): fires code slot events when a PIN is used
527	        - LockUserChange (event 4): pushes occupancy updates to coordinator
528	          when credentials are added, modified, or cleared
529	
530	        Called by BaseLock.subscribe_push_updates(). On failure, the
531	        reconnect handlers will retry when the integration reloads.
532	        """
533	        if self._push_unsubs:
534	            return
535	
536	        client = self._get_matter_client()
537	        node = self._get_matter_node()
538	        node_id = node.node_id if node else None
539	        if not client or node_id is None:
540	            raise LockDisconnected(
541	                f"Matter client or node ID unavailable for {self.lock.entity_id}"
542	            )
543	
544	        self._register_push_unsub(
545	            client.subscribe_events(
546	                callback=self._on_node_event,
547	                event_filter=EventType.NODE_EVENT,
548	                node_filter=node_id,
549	            )
550	        )
551	        LOGGER.debug(
552	            "Lock %s: subscribed to Matter events (node %s)",
553	            self.lock.entity_id,
554	            node_id,
555	        )
556	
557	    @callback
558	    def teardown_push_subscription(self) -> None:
559	        """Unsubscribe from Matter DoorLock cluster events."""
560	        self._clear_push_unsubs()
561	
562	    @callback
563	    def _on_node_event(self, _event: Any, node_event: Any) -> None:
564	        """Dispatch DoorLock cluster events to the appropriate handler."""
565	        if getattr(node_event, "cluster_id", None) != _DOOR_LOCK_CLUSTER_ID:
566	            return
567	
568	        event_id = getattr(node_event, "event_id", None)
569	        if event_id == _LOCK_OPERATION_EVENT_ID:
570	            self._handle_lock_operation(node_event)
571	        elif event_id == _LOCK_USER_CHANGE_EVENT_ID:
572	            self._handle_lock_user_change(node_event)
573	        else:
574	            LOGGER.debug(
575	                "Lock %s: unhandled DoorLock event_id=%s",
576	                self.lock.entity_id,
577	                event_id,
578	            )
579	
580	    @callback
581	    def _handle_lock_operation(self, node_event: Any) -> None:
582	        """
583	        Handle LockOperation events (event ID 2).
584	
585	        Fires a code slot event when a PIN credential is used to lock/unlock.
586	        Only PIN credentials (credentialType=1) trigger the event — other
587	        credential types (RFID, fingerprint, etc.) are ignored.
588	        """
589	        data: dict[str, Any] = getattr(node_event, "data", None) or {}
590	        credentials = data.get("credentials")
591	        lock_operation_type = data.get("lockOperationType")
592	
593	        # Must have credentials with a PIN type to fire
594	        if not credentials:
595	            return
596	
597	        # Find the PIN credential index (credentialType 1 = PIN)
598	        code_slot: int | None = None
599	        for cred in credentials:
600	            if isinstance(cred, dict) and cred.get("credentialType") == 1:
601	                code_slot = cred.get("credentialIndex")
602	                break
603	
604	        if code_slot is None:
605	            return
606	
607	        # lockOperationType: 0=Lock, 1=Unlock, 2=NonAccessUserEvent, 3=ForcedUserEvent, 4=Unlatch
608	        if lock_operation_type == 0:
609	            to_locked = True
610	        elif lock_operation_type == 1:
611	            to_locked = False
612	        else:
613	            to_locked = None
614	
615	        LOGGER.debug(
616	            "Lock %s: LockOperation event — slot=%s, locked=%s",
617	            self.lock.entity_id,
618	            code_slot,
619	            to_locked,
620	        )
621	
622	        self.async_fire_code_slot_event(
623	            code_slot=code_slot,
624	            to_locked=to_locked,
625	            action_text="locked"
626	            if to_locked
627	            else "unlocked"
628	            if to_locked is False
629	            else "operated",
630	            source_data=data,
631	        )
632	
633	    @callback
634	    def _handle_lock_user_change(self, node_event: Any) -> None:
635	        """
636	        Handle LockUserChange events (event ID 4).
637	
638	        Pushes occupancy updates to the coordinator when a PIN credential is
639	        added, modified, or cleared. This provides real-time change detection
640	        without waiting for the next poll cycle.
641	
642	        Only PIN credentials (LockDataType=6) are handled. The DataIndex field
643	        maps to the credential index (code slot number).
644	        """
645	        data: dict[str, Any] = getattr(node_event, "data", None) or {}
646	
647	        if data.get("lockDataType") != _LOCK_DATA_TYPE_PIN:
648	            return
649	
650	        raw_index = data.get("dataIndex")
651	        if raw_index is None:
652	            return
653	        code_slot = parse_slot_num(raw_index)
654	        if code_slot is None:
655	            LOGGER.warning(
656	                "Lock %s: LockUserChange has non-integer dataIndex %r, ignoring",
657	                self.lock.entity_id,
658	                raw_index,
659	            )
660	            return
661	
662	        operation = data.get("dataOperationType")
663	
664	        if operation == _DATA_OP_CLEAR:
665	            resolved = SlotCredential.empty()
666	        elif operation in (_DATA_OP_ADD, _DATA_OP_MODIFY):
667	            resolved = SlotCredential.unreadable()
668	        else:
669	            LOGGER.debug(
670	                "Lock %s: LockUserChange event with unknown operation %s for slot %s",
671	                self.lock.entity_id,
672	                operation,
673	                code_slot,
674	            )
675	            return
676	
677	        LOGGER.debug(
678	            "Lock %s: LockUserChange event — slot=%s, operation=%s, resolved=%s",
679	            self.lock.entity_id,
680	            code_slot,
681	            operation,
682	            resolved,
683	        )
684	
685	        self._push_credential_update(code_slot, resolved)
686	
687	    async def async_hard_refresh_codes(self) -> dict[int, SlotCredential]:
688	        """
689	        Perform a hard refresh and return all slot credentials.
690	
691	        Matter has no on-device cache to invalidate. This re-fetches the current
692	        user list fresh from the lock and projects it through the base
693	        async_get_usercodes() projection (managed slots as empty, occupied
694	        Personal Identification Number slots as unreadable).
695	        """
696	        return await self.async_get_usercodes()
697

"""
Matter lock provider.

Handles PIN credential management via Matter lock helpers.
PINs are write-only: occupied slots report ``SlotCredential.unreadable()``,
cleared slots report ``SlotCredential.empty()``. Subscribes to DoorLock
cluster events via the push framework for code slot tracking (LockOperation)
and occupancy updates (LockUserChange).
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from dataclasses import dataclass
from datetime import timedelta
from typing import Any, Literal

from matter_server.common.models import EventType

from homeassistant.components.matter.helpers import (
    get_matter,
    get_node_from_device_entry,
)
from homeassistant.components.matter.lock_helpers import (
    SetCredentialFailedError,
    clear_lock_credential,
    clear_lock_user,
    get_lock_credential_status,
    get_lock_info,
    get_lock_users,
    set_lock_credential,
    set_lock_user,
)
from homeassistant.config_entries import ConfigEntry
from homeassistant.core import callback
from homeassistant.exceptions import HomeAssistantError, ServiceValidationError

from ..domain.credentials import (
    Credential,
    CredentialRef,
    CredentialType,
    CredentialTypeCapability,
    LockCapabilities,
    SetUserResult,
    User,
)
from ..domain.exceptions import (
    CodeRejectedError,
    DuplicateCodeError,
    LockCodeManagerProviderError,
    LockDisconnected,
    LockOperationFailed,
)
from ..domain.models import SlotCredential
from ._base import BaseLock
from ._util import parse_slot_num
from .const import LOGGER

# DoorLock cluster ID (0x0101 = 257)
_DOOR_LOCK_CLUSTER_ID = 257

# DoorLock cluster event IDs
_LOCK_OPERATION_EVENT_ID = 2
_LOCK_USER_CHANGE_EVENT_ID = 4

# LockUserChange LockDataType values
_LOCK_DATA_TYPE_PIN = 6

# LockUserChange DataOperationType values
_DATA_OP_ADD = 0
_DATA_OP_CLEAR = 1
_DATA_OP_MODIFY = 2


@dataclass(repr=False, eq=False)
class MatterLock(BaseLock):
    """Class to represent a Matter lock."""

    @property
    def domain(self) -> str:
        """Return integration domain."""
        return "matter"

    @property
    def supports_push(self) -> bool:
        """
        Return whether this lock supports push-based updates.

        Matter locks push occupancy changes via LockUserChange events.
        PINs are still write-only (values are never pushed), but slot
        occupancy (unreadable/empty credentials) is pushed in real time.
        """
        return True

    @property
    def usercode_scan_interval(self) -> timedelta:
        """Return scan interval for usercodes."""
        return timedelta(minutes=5)

    @property
    def hard_refresh_interval(self) -> timedelta | None:
        """
        Return interval between hard refreshes for drift detection.

        Matter locks support push events for local changes, but API-initiated
        changes bypass push notifications. Periodic hard refresh catches drift
        from external tools or missed events.
        """
        return timedelta(hours=1)

    @property
    def supports_native_users(self) -> bool:
        """
        Return True — Matter locks expose the full User/Credential model.

        Matter's DoorLock cluster manages users and credentials as distinct
        entities, so the base orchestration runs the user-first lifecycle
        (create/update user, then write its Personal Identification Number
        credential; delete the user when its last credential is removed).
        """
        return True

    def _get_matter_node(self) -> Any | None:
        """
        Get the MatterNode for this lock from the Matter integration.

        Uses the Matter integration's helper to resolve the node from the
        device entry, which correctly handles the device identifier format.
        Returns the node object with .node_id and access to the client.
        """
        if not self.device_entry:
            return None
        try:
            return get_node_from_device_entry(self.hass, self.device_entry)
        except Exception as err:
            LOGGER.debug(
                "Failed to resolve Matter node for %s: %s",
                self.lock.entity_id,
                err,
            )
            return None

    def _get_matter_client(self) -> Any | None:
        """Get the MatterClient via the Matter integration helper."""
        try:
            return get_matter(self.hass).matter_client
        except Exception as err:
            LOGGER.debug(
                "Failed to get Matter client for %s: %s",
                self.lock.entity_id,
                err,
            )
            return None

    def _require_client_and_node(self) -> tuple[Any, Any]:
        """Get client and node, raising LockDisconnected if unavailable."""
        client = self._get_matter_client()
        node = self._get_matter_node()
        if not client or not node:
            raise LockDisconnected(
                f"Matter client or node unavailable for {self.lock.entity_id}"
            )
        return client, node

    # -- Credential primitives -----------------------------------------------

    async def async_get_users(self) -> list[User]:
        """
        Read every user and their Personal Identification Number credentials from the lock.

        Matter PINs are write-only: each occupied credential slot is projected to
        SlotCredential.unreadable(). Non-PIN credentials (for example RFID) are
        filtered out because the coordinator and sync manager only manage PIN slots.
        """
        client, node = self._require_client_and_node()
        try:
            lock_data = await get_lock_users(client, node)
        except HomeAssistantError as err:
            raise LockDisconnected(
                f"Matter get_lock_users failed for {self.lock.entity_id}: {err}"
            ) from err

        # A for-loop (not a comprehension) so the int-or-None user_index and
        # credential index are narrowed by explicit guards before use: the
        # Matter helper types both as ``int | None``.
        users: list[User] = []
        for raw_user in lock_data.get("users", []):
            user_index = raw_user.get("user_index")
            if user_index is None:
                continue
            pin_credentials: list[Credential] = []
            for cred in raw_user.get("credentials", []):
                slot = cred.get("index")
                if cred.get("type") != "pin" or slot is None:
                    continue
                pin_credentials.append(
                    Credential(
                        type=CredentialType.PIN,
                        slot=slot,
                        state=SlotCredential.unreadable(),
                    )
                )
            users.append(
                User(
                    user_id=user_index,
                    name=raw_user.get("user_name"),
                    active=True,
                    credentials=pin_credentials,
                )
            )
        return users

    async def async_get_capabilities(self) -> LockCapabilities:
        """
        Read lock capabilities from the Matter DoorLock cluster.

        Maps the get_lock_info result to a platform-neutral LockCapabilities.
        Only the Personal Identification Number credential type is surfaced
        when the lock advertises PIN support. None capacity fields default
        to 0 (unknown capacity) rather than raising.
        """
        client, node = self._require_client_and_node()
        try:
            info = await get_lock_info(client, node)
        except HomeAssistantError as err:
            raise LockDisconnected(
                f"Matter get_lock_info failed for {self.lock.entity_id}: {err}"
            ) from err

        credential_types: dict[CredentialType, CredentialTypeCapability] = {}
        if "pin" in (info.get("supported_credential_types") or []):
            credential_types[CredentialType.PIN] = CredentialTypeCapability(
                num_slots=info.get("max_pin_users") or 0,
                min_length=info.get("min_pin_length") or 0,
                max_length=info.get("max_pin_length") or 0,
                supports_learn=False,
            )

        return LockCapabilities(
            supports_user_management=bool(info.get("supports_user_management")),
            max_users=info.get("max_users") or 0,
            credential_types=credential_types,
        )

    async def async_set_user(self, user: User) -> SetUserResult:
        """
        Create or update a lock user, returning whether it was newly created.

        The user.user_id here is the slot (credential_index). Matter allocates
        user_index independently: you cannot create a user at a chosen index —
        set_lock_user raises UserSlotEmptyError when user_index is given for an
        empty slot. So the flow is:

        1. Check the credential slot's current owner via get_lock_credential_status.
        2. If the slot is occupied (UPDATE): call set_lock_user with the existing
           user_index from the status response.
        3. If the slot is empty (CREATE): call set_lock_user with user_index=None
           so Matter auto-allocates a free user slot and returns the new index.

        The returned SetUserResult carries the real Matter user_index, which the
        base orchestration passes into async_set_credential as the user_id.
        """
        client, node = self._require_client_and_node()
        try:
            status = await get_lock_credential_status(
                client,
                node,
                credential_type="pin",
                credential_index=user.user_id,
            )
        except HomeAssistantError as err:
            raise LockDisconnected(
                f"Matter get_lock_credential_status failed for "
                f"{self.lock.entity_id}: {err}"
            ) from err

        credential_exists = status.get("credential_exists", False)
        existing_user_index = status.get("user_index")

        if credential_exists and existing_user_index is not None:
            # UPDATE: the slot is occupied — modify the existing user record.
            try:
                await set_lock_user(
                    client,
                    node,
                    user_index=existing_user_index,
                    user_name=user.name,
                )
            except ServiceValidationError as err:
                raise LockOperationFailed(
                    f"Matter set_lock_user rejected input for "
                    f"{self.lock.entity_id}: {err}"
                ) from err
            except HomeAssistantError as err:
                raise LockDisconnected(
                    f"Matter set_lock_user failed for {self.lock.entity_id}: {err}"
                ) from err
            return SetUserResult(user_id=existing_user_index, created=False)

        # CREATE: the slot is empty — let Matter auto-allocate a user_index.
        try:
            result = await set_lock_user(
                client,
                node,
                user_index=None,
                user_name=user.name,
            )
        except ServiceValidationError as err:
            raise LockOperationFailed(
                f"Matter set_lock_user rejected input for {self.lock.entity_id}: {err}"
            ) from err
        except HomeAssistantError as err:
            raise LockDisconnected(
                f"Matter set_lock_user failed for {self.lock.entity_id}: {err}"
            ) from err
        return SetUserResult(user_id=result["user_index"], created=True)

    async def async_delete_user(self, user_id: int) -> None:
        """
        Delete a lock user and all of its credentials.

        The Matter DoorLock ClearUser command also clears all associated
        credentials and schedules for the user per the Matter specification.
        """
        client, node = self._require_client_and_node()
        try:
            await clear_lock_user(client, node, user_id)
        except HomeAssistantError as err:
            raise LockDisconnected(
                f"Matter clear_lock_user failed for {self.lock.entity_id}: {err}"
            ) from err

    async def _send_set_credential(
        self,
        client: Any,
        node: Any,
        code_slot: int,
        pin: str,
        user_id: int,
    ) -> None:
        """
        Send set_lock_credential to the lock for the given slot, PIN, and user.

        Raises SetCredentialFailedError on lock rejection,
        CodeRejectedError on validation failure,
        LockDisconnected on communication failure.
        """
        try:
            await set_lock_credential(
                client,
                node,
                credential_type="pin",
                credential_data=pin,
                credential_index=code_slot,
                user_index=user_id,
            )
        except SetCredentialFailedError:
            raise
        except ServiceValidationError as err:
            # Bad Personal Identification Number / unsupported type -> the lock
            # rejects the value; surface as a code rejection.
            raise CodeRejectedError(
                code_slot=code_slot,
                lock_entity_id=self.lock.entity_id,
                reason=str(err),
            ) from err
        except HomeAssistantError as err:
            # Transport / endpoint failure -> route to the retry path.
            raise LockDisconnected(
                f"Matter set_lock_credential failed for {self.lock.entity_id}: {err}"
            ) from err

    async def async_set_credential(
        self,
        user_id: int,
        credential: Credential,
        *,
        name: str | None,
        source: Literal["sync", "direct"],
    ) -> bool:
        """
        Write a Personal Identification Number credential to the lock.

        Raises CodeRejectedError when the credential has no readable value
        (for example when projecting an already-unreadable slot — the lock
        requires a concrete PIN string). Handles the duplicate-slot restart
        case for sync sources by clearing and retrying once.

        The base orchestration skips coordinator refresh for push providers.
        Matter does not emit LockUserChange for LCM-initiated writes, so an
        optimistic push is required to keep the coordinator current.
        """
        if credential.readable_pin is None:
            raise CodeRejectedError(
                code_slot=credential.slot,
                lock_entity_id=self.lock.entity_id,
                reason="credential has no readable Personal Identification Number value",
            )

        client, node = self._require_client_and_node()
        pin = credential.readable_pin
        slot = credential.slot

        try:
            await self._send_set_credential(client, node, slot, pin, user_id)
        except SetCredentialFailedError as err:
            status = (err.translation_placeholders or {}).get("status", "")
            if status != "duplicate":
                raise CodeRejectedError(
                    code_slot=slot,
                    lock_entity_id=self.lock.entity_id,
                    reason=str(err),
                ) from err
            if source != "sync":
                raise DuplicateCodeError(
                    code_slot=slot,
                    lock_entity_id=self.lock.entity_id,
                ) from err

            # Sync duplicate: clear and retry once
            LOGGER.debug(
                "Lock %s: duplicate on slot %s, clearing and retrying",
                self.lock.entity_id,
                slot,
            )
            await clear_lock_credential(
                client, node, credential_type="pin", credential_index=slot
            )
            try:
                await self._send_set_credential(client, node, slot, pin, user_id)
            except SetCredentialFailedError as retry_err:
                retry_status = (retry_err.translation_placeholders or {}).get(
                    "status", ""
                )
                if retry_status == "duplicate":
                    raise DuplicateCodeError(
                        code_slot=slot,
                        lock_entity_id=self.lock.entity_id,
                    ) from retry_err
                raise CodeRejectedError(
                    code_slot=slot,
                    lock_entity_id=self.lock.entity_id,
                    reason=str(retry_err),
                ) from retry_err

        self._push_credential_update(slot, SlotCredential.unreadable())
        return True

    async def async_delete_credential(self, ref: CredentialRef) -> bool:
        """
        Clear a Personal Identification Number credential from the lock.

        Returns True on success. Pushes SlotCredential.empty() to the
        coordinator immediately because Matter does not emit LockUserChange
        for LCM-initiated clears.
        """
        client, node = self._require_client_and_node()
        try:
            await clear_lock_credential(
                client,
                node,
                credential_type="pin",
                credential_index=ref.slot,
            )
        except ServiceValidationError as err:
            raise LockOperationFailed(
                f"Matter clear_lock_credential rejected input for "
                f"{self.lock.entity_id}: {err}"
            ) from err
        except HomeAssistantError as err:
            raise LockDisconnected(
                f"Matter clear_lock_credential failed for {self.lock.entity_id}: {err}"
            ) from err

        self._push_credential_update(ref.slot, SlotCredential.empty())
        return True

    async def async_setup(self, config_entry: ConfigEntry) -> None:
        """Validate the lock supports Matter user management."""
        client, node = self._require_client_and_node()
        try:
            lock_info = await get_lock_info(client, node)
        except ServiceValidationError as err:
            raise LockCodeManagerProviderError(
                f"Matter get_lock_info rejected input for {self.lock.entity_id}: {err}"
            ) from err
        except HomeAssistantError as err:
            raise LockDisconnected(
                f"Matter get_lock_info failed for {self.lock.entity_id}: {err}"
            ) from err
        if not lock_info.get("supports_user_management"):
            raise LockCodeManagerProviderError(
                f"Matter lock {self.lock.entity_id} does not support user management"
            )
        if "pin" not in (lock_info.get("supported_credential_types") or []):
            raise LockCodeManagerProviderError(
                f"Matter lock {self.lock.entity_id} does not support PIN credentials"
            )
        LOGGER.debug(
            "Matter lock %s setup complete: %s",
            self.lock.entity_id,
            lock_info,
        )

    async def async_is_device_available(self) -> bool:
        """Return whether the Matter lock device is available for commands."""
        try:
            client, node = self._require_client_and_node()
            await get_lock_info(client, node)
        except (LockCodeManagerProviderError, HomeAssistantError) as err:
            LOGGER.debug(
                "Lock %s: availability check failed: %s",
                self.lock.entity_id,
                err,
            )
            return False
        return True

    # -- Event subscription via push framework --------------------------------

    @callback
    def setup_push_subscription(self) -> None:
        """
        Subscribe to Matter DoorLock cluster events.

        Handles two event types:
        - LockOperation (event 2): fires code slot events when a PIN is used
        - LockUserChange (event 4): pushes occupancy updates to coordinator
          when credentials are added, modified, or cleared

        Called by BaseLock.subscribe_push_updates(). On failure, the
        reconnect handlers will retry when the integration reloads.
        """
        if self._push_unsubs:
            return

        client = self._get_matter_client()
        node = self._get_matter_node()
        node_id = node.node_id if node else None
        if not client or node_id is None:
            raise LockDisconnected(
                f"Matter client or node ID unavailable for {self.lock.entity_id}"
            )

        self._register_push_unsub(
            client.subscribe_events(
                callback=self._on_node_event,
                event_filter=EventType.NODE_EVENT,
                node_filter=node_id,
            )
        )
        LOGGER.debug(
            "Lock %s: subscribed to Matter events (node %s)",
            self.lock.entity_id,
            node_id,
        )

    @callback
    def teardown_push_subscription(self) -> None:
        """Unsubscribe from Matter DoorLock cluster events."""
        self._clear_push_unsubs()

    @callback
    def _on_node_event(self, _event: Any, node_event: Any) -> None:
        """Dispatch DoorLock cluster events to the appropriate handler."""
        if getattr(node_event, "cluster_id", None) != _DOOR_LOCK_CLUSTER_ID:
            return

        event_id = getattr(node_event, "event_id", None)
        if event_id == _LOCK_OPERATION_EVENT_ID:
            self._handle_lock_operation(node_event)
        elif event_id == _LOCK_USER_CHANGE_EVENT_ID:
            self._handle_lock_user_change(node_event)
        else:
            LOGGER.debug(
                "Lock %s: unhandled DoorLock event_id=%s",
                self.lock.entity_id,
                event_id,
            )

    @callback
    def _handle_lock_operation(self, node_event: Any) -> None:
        """
        Handle LockOperation events (event ID 2).

        Fires a code slot event when a PIN credential is used to lock/unlock.
        Only PIN credentials (credentialType=1) trigger the event — other
        credential types (RFID, fingerprint, etc.) are ignored.
        """
        data: dict[str, Any] = getattr(node_event, "data", None) or {}
        credentials = data.get("credentials")
        lock_operation_type = data.get("lockOperationType")

        # Must have credentials with a PIN type to fire
        if not credentials:
            return

        # Find the PIN credential index (credentialType 1 = PIN)
        code_slot: int | None = None
        for cred in credentials:
            if isinstance(cred, dict) and cred.get("credentialType") == 1:
                code_slot = cred.get("credentialIndex")
                break

        if code_slot is None:
            return

        # lockOperationType: 0=Lock, 1=Unlock, 2=NonAccessUserEvent, 3=ForcedUserEvent, 4=Unlatch
        if lock_operation_type == 0:
            to_locked = True
        elif lock_operation_type == 1:
            to_locked = False
        else:
            to_locked = None

        LOGGER.debug(
            "Lock %s: LockOperation event — slot=%s, locked=%s",
            self.lock.entity_id,
            code_slot,
            to_locked,
        )

        self.async_fire_code_slot_event(
            code_slot=code_slot,
            to_locked=to_locked,
            action_text="locked"
            if to_locked
            else "unlocked"
            if to_locked is False
            else "operated",
            source_data=data,
        )

    @callback
    def _handle_lock_user_change(self, node_event: Any) -> None:
        """
        Handle LockUserChange events (event ID 4).

        Pushes occupancy updates to the coordinator when a PIN credential is
        added, modified, or cleared. This provides real-time change detection
        without waiting for the next poll cycle.

        Only PIN credentials (LockDataType=6) are handled. The DataIndex field
        maps to the credential index (code slot number).
        """
        data: dict[str, Any] = getattr(node_event, "data", None) or {}

        if data.get("lockDataType") != _LOCK_DATA_TYPE_PIN:
            return

        raw_index = data.get("dataIndex")
        if raw_index is None:
            return
        code_slot = parse_slot_num(raw_index)
        if code_slot is None:
            LOGGER.warning(
                "Lock %s: LockUserChange has non-integer dataIndex %r, ignoring",
                self.lock.entity_id,
                raw_index,
            )
            return

        operation = data.get("dataOperationType")

        if operation == _DATA_OP_CLEAR:
            resolved = SlotCredential.empty()
        elif operation in (_DATA_OP_ADD, _DATA_OP_MODIFY):
            resolved = SlotCredential.unreadable()
        else:
            LOGGER.debug(
                "Lock %s: LockUserChange event with unknown operation %s for slot %s",
                self.lock.entity_id,
                operation,
                code_slot,
            )
            return

        LOGGER.debug(
            "Lock %s: LockUserChange event — slot=%s, operation=%s, resolved=%s",
            self.lock.entity_id,
            code_slot,
            operation,
            resolved,
        )

        self._push_credential_update(code_slot, resolved)

    async def async_hard_refresh_codes(self) -> dict[int, SlotCredential]:
        """
        Perform a hard refresh and return all slot credentials.

        Matter has no on-device cache to invalidate. This re-fetches the current
        user list fresh from the lock and projects it through the base
        async_get_usercodes() projection (managed slots as empty, occupied
        Personal Identification Number slots as unreadable).
        """
        return await self.async_get_usercodes()

1	"""
2	Local Akuvox lock provider.
3	
4	Akuvox door controllers manage access codes as *users* identified by an
5	internal device ID, not by numeric slot numbers. This provider bridges
6	that gap by tagging user names with a slot prefix in the format
7	``[LCM:<slot>] <friendly name>``. Pre-existing users discovered on the
8	device are automatically tagged and assigned to the next available slot
9	number.
10	
11	All operations go through the Home Assistant ``local_akuvox`` integration
12	services (``list_users``, ``add_user``, ``modify_user``, ``delete_user``).
13	"""
14	
15	from __future__ import annotations
16	
17	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
18	from datetime import timedelta
19	from typing import Any, Literal
20	
21	from homeassistant.config_entries import ConfigEntry
22	
23	from ..domain.credentials import Credential, CredentialRef, User, user_from_slot
24	from ..domain.exceptions import (
25	    LockCodeManagerProviderError,
26	    LockDisconnected,
27	    LockOperationFailed,
28	)
29	from ..domain.models import SlotCredential
30	from ._base import BaseLock
31	from ._util import make_tagged_name as _make_tagged_name, parse_tag as _parse_tag
32	from .const import LOGGER
33	
34	AKUVOX_DOMAIN = "local_akuvox"
35	
36	# Default schedule/relay values for Lock Code Manager-managed users.
37	# These are required by the Akuvox add_user service but are not
38	# meaningful for the code-slot abstraction.
39	_DEFAULT_SCHEDULE_IDS = "1001"  # "Always" schedule on Akuvox devices
40	_DEFAULT_LIFT_FLOOR_NUM = "1"
41	
42	# Akuvox firmware varies in how it marks local versus cloud users:
43	#   A08S / E18C: source_type "1" = local, "2" = cloud, user_type "0" for both
44	#   X916:        source_type None for all, user_type "-1" = local, "0" = cloud
45	_LOCAL_SOURCE_TYPE = "1"
46	_LOCAL_USER_TYPE = "-1"
47	
48	
49	def _is_local_user(user: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
50	    """Return True if *user* was created locally on the device."""
51	    source_type = user.get("source_type")
52	    if source_type:
53	        return str(source_type) == _LOCAL_SOURCE_TYPE
54	    # source_type absent -- fall back to user_type (X916 pattern)
55	    return str(user.get("user_type", "")) == _LOCAL_USER_TYPE
56	
57	
58	@dataclass(repr=False, eq=False)
59	class AkuvoxLock(BaseLock):
60	    """
61	    Local Akuvox lock provider implementation.
62	
63	    Users on Akuvox controllers are identified by a device-internal ID
64	    and a name, not by slot numbers. This provider assigns virtual slot
65	    numbers by embedding a ``[LCM:<slot>]`` tag in each user's name.
66	
67	    PINs are readable from the device, so occupied slots report the actual
68	    PIN value. Cleared slots report ``SlotCredential.empty()``.
69	    """
70	
71	    # Tracks whether the initial auto-tag pass already ran for this
72	    # provider instance. Skips re-tagging on reconnects so a drifted
73	    # device list does not produce double-tag / rename storms. Reset
74	    # naturally when the provider instance is recreated on full reload.
75	    _tagged_once: bool = field(default=False, init=False)
76	
77	    @property
78	    def domain(self) -> str:
79	        """Return integration domain."""
80	        return AKUVOX_DOMAIN
81	
82	    @property
83	    def supports_code_slot_events(self) -> bool:
84	        """Return whether this lock supports code slot events."""
85	        return False
86	
87	    @property
88	    def usercode_scan_interval(self) -> timedelta:
89	        """Return scan interval for usercodes."""
90	        return timedelta(minutes=2)
91	
92	    async def _async_list_users(self) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
93	        """
94	        Call ``local_akuvox.list_users`` and return the user list.
95	
96	        Returns a list of user dicts with keys: id, name, user_id,
97	        private_pin, card_code, schedule_relay, lift_floor_num, etc.
98	        """
99	        entity_id = self.lock.entity_id
100	        response = await self.async_call_service(
101	            AKUVOX_DOMAIN,
102	            "list_users",
103	            service_data={},
104	            target={"entity_id": entity_id},
105	            return_response=True,
106	        )
107	
108	        if not isinstance(response, dict):
109	            raise LockCodeManagerProviderError(
110	                f"Malformed list_users response from {entity_id}: "
111	                f"expected dict, got {type(response).__name__}"
112	            )
113	
114	        # Platform entity services wrap the response per entity_id.
115	        entity_response = response.get(entity_id, response)
116	        if not isinstance(entity_response, dict):
117	            raise LockCodeManagerProviderError(
118	                f"Malformed list_users entity response from {entity_id}: "
119	                f"expected dict, got {type(entity_response).__name__}"
120	            )
121	        return entity_response.get("users", [])
122	
123	    async def _async_add_user(self, name: str, pin: str) -> None:
124	        """Add a new user with the given name and PIN."""
125	        entity_id = self.lock.entity_id
126	        await self.async_call_service(
127	            AKUVOX_DOMAIN,
128	            "add_user",
129	            service_data={
130	                "name": name,
131	                "private_pin": pin,
132	                "schedules": _DEFAULT_SCHEDULE_IDS,
133	                "lift_floor_num": _DEFAULT_LIFT_FLOOR_NUM,
134	            },
135	            target={"entity_id": entity_id},
136	        )
137	
138	    async def _async_modify_user(
139	        self,
140	        device_user_id: str,
141	        *,
142	        name: str | None = None,
143	        pin: str | None = None,
144	    ) -> None:
145	        """Modify an existing user."""
146	        entity_id = self.lock.entity_id
147	        service_data: dict[str, Any] = {"id": device_user_id}
148	        if name is not None:
149	            service_data["name"] = name
150	        if pin is not None:
151	            service_data["private_pin"] = pin
152	        await self.async_call_service(
153	            AKUVOX_DOMAIN,
154	            "modify_user",
155	            service_data=service_data,
156	            target={"entity_id": entity_id},
157	        )
158	
159	    async def _async_delete_user(self, device_user_id: str) -> None:
160	        """Delete a user by their device-internal ID."""
161	        entity_id = self.lock.entity_id
162	        await self.async_call_service(
163	            AKUVOX_DOMAIN,
164	            "delete_user",
165	            service_data={"id": device_user_id},
166	            target={"entity_id": entity_id},
167	        )
168	
169	    async def async_setup(self, config_entry: ConfigEntry) -> None:
170	        """
171	        Set up lock by tagging any pre-existing unmanaged users.
172	
173	        Idempotent: the tag pass runs only once per provider instance to
174	        prevent double-tag / rename storms on reconnect (the device list
175	        may have drifted since the previous load).
176	        """
177	        await super().async_setup(config_entry)
178	        if self._tagged_once:
179	            return
180	        await self._async_tag_unmanaged_users()
181	        self._tagged_once = True
182	
183	    async def async_hard_refresh_codes(self) -> dict[int, SlotCredential]:
184	        """
185	        Re-tag unmanaged users, then return all codes.
186	
187	        Hard refresh is an explicit drift-detection request, so the
188	        setup-time idempotency gate does not apply.
189	        """
190	        managed_slots = self.managed_slots
191	        if managed_slots:
192	            async with self._serialize_sequence():
193	                await self._async_run_tag_pass(managed_slots)
194	        return await self.async_get_usercodes()
195	
196	    async def _async_tag_unmanaged_users(self) -> None:
197	        """
198	        Discover untagged local users and tag them with a slot number.
199	
200	        Untagged local users that have a PIN are assigned to the next
201	        available managed slot and their names are updated on the device
202	        to include the ``[LCM:<slot>]`` tag via ``modify_user``. The
203	        list/modify sequence runs under the sequence lock so concurrent
204	        set/clear/hard-refresh callers do not interleave their own
205	        multi-step writes.
206	        """
207	        managed_slots = self.managed_slots
208	        if not managed_slots:
209	            return
210	
211	        async with self._serialize_sequence():
212	            await self._async_run_tag_pass(managed_slots)
213	
214	    async def _async_run_tag_pass(self, managed_slots: set[int]) -> None:
215	        """
216	        Body of the tag pass; caller holds the sequence lock.
217	
218	        Per-user ``LockDisconnected`` is logged and the loop continues so
219	        any remaining users still get a chance to tag, but the disconnect
220	        is re-raised at the end. ``async_setup`` then leaves
221	        ``_tagged_once`` False so the reconnect path retries once the lock
222	        is reachable. ``LockOperationFailed`` is a per-user condition and
223	        does not gate idempotency.
224	        """
225	        users = await self._async_list_users()
226	
227	        assigned_slots: set[int] = set()
228	        # (device_user_id, pin, original_name) for untagged users needing assignment
229	        untagged: list[tuple[str, str, str]] = []
230	
231	        for user in users:
232	            if not _is_local_user(user):
233	                continue
234	            name = user.get("name", "")
235	            pin = user.get("private_pin", "")
236	            device_id = str(user.get("id", ""))
237	            slot_num, _ = _parse_tag(name)
238	
239	            if slot_num is not None:
240	                assigned_slots.add(slot_num)
241	            elif pin:
242	                untagged.append((device_id, pin, name))
243	
244	        available = sorted(managed_slots - assigned_slots)
245	        first_disconnect: LockDisconnected | None = None
246	        for device_id, _pin, original_name in untagged:
247	            if not available:
248	                LOGGER.debug(
249	                    "Lock %s: no managed slot available for untagged user '%s'; "
250	                    "leaving untouched",
251	                    self.lock.entity_id,
252	                    original_name,
253	                )
254	                break
255	
256	            slot_num = available[0]
257	            tagged_name = _make_tagged_name(slot_num, original_name)
258	            try:
259	                await self._async_modify_user(device_id, name=tagged_name)
260	            except (LockDisconnected, LockOperationFailed) as err:
261	                LOGGER.error(
262	                    "Lock %s: failed to tag user '%s' for slot %d: %s",
263	                    self.lock.entity_id,
264	                    original_name,
265	                    slot_num,
266	                    err,
267	                )
268	                if first_disconnect is None and isinstance(err, LockDisconnected):
269	                    first_disconnect = err
270	                continue
271	
272	            available.pop(0)
273	            LOGGER.debug(
274	                "Lock %s: tagged user '%s' (id=%s) as slot %d: '%s'",
275	                self.lock.entity_id,
276	                original_name,
277	                device_id,
278	                slot_num,
279	                tagged_name,
280	            )
281	
282	        if first_disconnect is not None:
283	            raise LockDisconnected(
284	                f"Lock {self.lock.entity_id}: disconnect during tag pass; "
285	                "will be retried on reconnect"
286	            ) from first_disconnect
287	
288	    async def async_get_users(self) -> list[User]:
289	        """
290	        Return users by reading tagged local users from the Akuvox device.
291	
292	        Users bearing a ``[LCM:<slot>]`` tag in their name are mapped to the
293	        embedded slot number. Only reads and classifies; auto-tagging of
294	        unmanaged users is handled separately by ``_async_tag_unmanaged_users()``.
295	        Only users whose slot numbers fall within the managed set are returned.
296	        Personal Identification Numbers are readable on Akuvox, so occupied
297	        slots report the actual value.
298	        """
299	        managed_slots = self.managed_slots
300	        if not managed_slots:
301	            return []
302	
303	        users = await self._async_list_users()
304	
305	        slot_states: dict[int, SlotCredential] = dict.fromkeys(
306	            managed_slots, SlotCredential.empty()
307	        )
308	
309	        for user in users:
310	            if not _is_local_user(user):
311	                continue
312	            name = user.get("name", "")
313	            pin = user.get("private_pin", "")
314	            slot_num, _ = _parse_tag(name)
315	
316	            if slot_num is not None and slot_num in managed_slots:
317	                slot_states[slot_num] = (
318	                    SlotCredential.known(pin) if pin else SlotCredential.empty()
319	                )
320	
321	        LOGGER.debug(
322	            "Lock %s: %s managed slots, %s occupied",
323	            self.lock.entity_id,
324	            len(managed_slots),
325	            sum(1 for v in slot_states.values() if v.is_present),
326	        )
327	        return [user_from_slot(slot, state) for slot, state in slot_states.items()]
328	
329	    async def async_set_credential(
330	        self,
331	        user_id: int,
332	        credential: Credential,
333	        *,
334	        name: str | None,
335	        source: Literal["sync", "direct"],
336	    ) -> bool:
337	        """
338	        Set a Personal Identification Number credential on a slot.
339

341	        Identification Number (and optionally the name) is updated via
342	        ``modify_user``. Otherwise a new user is created via ``add_user``.
343	
344	        Returns True unconditionally because the Akuvox API does not
345	        indicate whether the value actually changed. The list/modify
346	        (or list/add) sequence runs under the sequence lock so concurrent
347	        callers cannot interleave their own multi-step writes against the
348	        same slot. Ignores ``user_id``; slot-only providers address the
349	        credential by slot.
350	        """
351	        code_slot = credential.slot
352	        usercode = credential.readable_pin or ""
353	        async with self._serialize_sequence():
354	            users = await self._async_list_users()
355	
356	            existing_device_id: str | None = None
357	            existing_friendly_name: str | None = None
358	            for user in users:
359	                if not _is_local_user(user):

363	                if parsed_slot == code_slot:

365	                    existing_friendly_name = friendly
366	                    break
367	
368	            effective_name = name or existing_friendly_name
369	            tagged_name = _make_tagged_name(code_slot, effective_name)
370	
371	            if existing_device_id:
372	                await self._async_modify_user(
373	                    existing_device_id, name=tagged_name, pin=usercode
374	                )
375	            else:
376	                await self._async_add_user(tagged_name, usercode)
377	
378	        LOGGER.debug(
379	            "Lock %s: set usercode on slot %s",
380	            self.lock.entity_id,
381	            code_slot,
382	        )
383	        return True
384	
385	    async def async_delete_credential(self, ref: CredentialRef) -> bool:
386	        """
387	        Delete the credential addressed by ``ref``; return whether it changed.
388	
389	        Returns True if a user was deleted, False if the slot was already

391	        the same reason ``async_set_credential`` does.
392	        """
